skill.name: project-map-builder
skill.description: Creates or updates PROJECT_MAP.md files for user-selected folders, with rules for scoped scanning, incremental updates, and clear uncertainty notes.
skill.summary: Build scoped project map documentation without scanning an entire repository by default.
skill.author: yunshu0909

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak cryptography, and high entropy indicators. Review found no prompt injection, malware intent, cryptography use, or executable code; the command alert is a Markdown example for scoped file listing.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Command Example
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Static analysis reported Ruby or shell backtick execution twice at line 54, but the line contains Markdown inline examples of file listing commands. The skill tells the assistant to ask for scope before scanning and does not define executable Ruby code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is inside a Markdown bullet and uses inline code formatting. It names common file listing commands and provides no executable script, interpolation, or user input sink.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Alert
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Static analysis reported a weak cryptographic algorithm at line 3, but that line is the skill description and references PROJECT_MAP.md documentation. No cryptographic operation or algorithm choice is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only relevant token is the .md filename suffix in descriptive text. I found no evidence of hashing, encryption, checksums, or cryptographic API usage.

low_findings.title: False Positive: High Entropy Heuristic
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Static analysis reported high entropy at line 1, but the file is plain Markdown with YAML front matter and Chinese prose. No encoded payload, binary content, or obfuscated instruction block was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the complete file showed readable documentation rather than hidden data. The entropy result is plausibly caused by multilingual UTF-8 text.

content.value_statement: Large repositories are hard to understand when directory purpose and file relationships are undocumented. This skill creates or updates PROJECT_MAP.md for selected folders using scoped scanning and incremental edits.
actual_capabilities: Asks the user to define one or more folders before scanning.
Generates PROJECT_MAP.md in the selected directory or project root.
Updates an existing PROJECT_MAP.md with incremental changes.
Documents entry points, key files, configuration, storage, tests, and risks.
Marks assumptions and unconfirmed behavior instead of presenting guesses as facts.
limitations: It depends on the assistant having permission to inspect the selected files.
It does not perform a full code audit or security review.
Very large scopes may require the user to narrow the folders.
Cross-directory relationships may be incomplete unless the user includes all relevant folders.

use_cases.title: Onboard to a Repository
use_cases.description: Create a concise map of important folders, entry points, and module responsibilities.

use_cases.title: Maintain Documentation
use_cases.description: Update an existing PROJECT_MAP.md after files are added, removed, or reorganized.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Review Scope
use_cases.description: Document selected folders before a refactor, migration, or design review.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Map
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a PROJECT_MAP.md for the src folder. Ask me before scanning anything outside that folder.

prompt_templates.title: Update Existing Documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Update the existing PROJECT_MAP.md for the api folder using only changes in that folder.

prompt_templates.title: Map Multiple Folders
prompt_templates.prompt: Build project maps for src, tests, and config. Ask whether to merge them or write separate files.

prompt_templates.title: Document Refactor Context
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the selected backend folders and update PROJECT_MAP.md with entry points, module relationships, risks, and uncertain areas.

output_examples.input: Create a PROJECT_MAP.md for the services folder.
output_examples.output: A scoped documentation file that lists the services folder, key entry points, important modules, configuration notes, and uncertain areas.

output_examples.input: Update the project map after adding a new auth module.
output_examples.output: An incremental update that preserves the existing structure and adds a current update note with the scanned range and changes.

output_examples.input: Map src and tests as separate documents.
output_examples.output: Two focused PROJECT_MAP.md files, each describing only its selected folder and avoiding cross-scope assumptions.

best_practices: Give exact folder paths before asking the assistant to scan.
Keep the first map small, then expand the scope when needed.
Review assumptions and uncertain sections before relying on the map.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for a full repository scan without confirming scope.
Do not treat the map as a substitute for code review.
Do not overwrite existing documentation when an incremental update is enough.

faq.question: Does this skill scan the whole repository by default?
faq.answer: No. It requires the user to specify folders before scanning.

faq.question: Can it update an existing PROJECT_MAP.md?
faq.answer: Yes. It reads the existing file and applies incremental updates for the selected scope.

faq.question: Where does it write the output?
faq.answer: For one folder, it writes PROJECT_MAP.md in that folder. For multiple folders, it asks whether to merge or split output.

faq.question: Does it read every file?
faq.answer: No. It lists files first and reads only key files needed to explain structure and relationships.

faq.question: Can it document unknown or unclear behavior?
faq.answer: Yes. It should label uncertain details as assumptions or unconfirmed behavior.

faq.question: Is this a security audit skill?
faq.answer: No. It documents project structure and may note risks, but it does not perform a full security review.
